results.title: Senior IT Security Analyst - Data & AI Platform job with Philip Morris International | 5532891
highlights:
Senior IT Security Analyst - Data & AI Platform job with Philip Morris International | 5532891
[...]
# Senior IT Security Analyst - Data & AI Platform
[...]
Employer Philip Morris International Location Albarraque, Portugal Salary USD 17,600.00 per month Closing date 6 Jul 2026
[...]
*The functional job title is Senior IT Analyst, Data Platform Security**The job can be based in Poland or Portugal.MAKE HISTORY WITH USAt PMI, we've chosen to do something incredible. We're totally transforming our business and building our future on smoke-free products with the power to deliver a smoke-free future.With huge change, comes huge opportunity. So, wherever you join us, you'll enjoy the freedom to dream up and deliver better, brighter solutions and you will have the space to move your career forward in many different areas/directions.We are looking for a Senior IT Security Analyst - Data & AI Platformto join our group of IT experts that manage a broad portfolio of systems, tools, and technologies that serve the Data & AI Platform for the entire enterprise.Over the past few years, we have built a modern Data & AI Platform to enable PMI's data-, analytics-, and AI-driven transformation. The platform is based on state-of-the-art architectures and technologies, covering data lake/lakehouse solutions, data ingestion and ELT, data management, data visualization and business intelligence, data science (advanced analytics and machine learning), as well as our latest AI stack, including the LLM Playground, Agentic Playground, and GenAI Platform.Our team is responsible for centrally building, evolving, and operating the global Data & AI Platform for the rest of PMI teams that develop data products, analytical solutions, and AI-driven applications on top of it.The Senior IT Security Analyst - Data & AI Platform will strengthen the existing Security team by acting as a 1st Line of Defense representative for information security. The role ensures that PMI's Data & AI Platform is deployed and operated securely, effectively bridging the speed and agility of Product teams (Architecture, Engineering, Support) with strict adherence to enterprise policies and standards governed by 2nd Line of Defense functions (Information Security, Privacy, Legal).JOIN USWHO ARE WE LOOKING FOR?
[...]
- Demonstrated knowledge and practical implementation experience with global AI security standards, regulations, and frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 27090, EU AI Act, OWASP AI Exchange)
[...]
- Experience supporting and securing AI/ML platforms built on a modern technology stack, spanning model development and hosting (e.g., Amazon SageMaker), foundation model consumption (e.g., Amazon Bedrock), LLM orchestration (e.g., LiteLLM), observability and evaluation (e.g., LangSmith), automation workflows (e.g., n8n), and third‑party AI services and enrichment tools (e.g., Tavily, Nova Lite)
[...]
- Proven experience defining and operationalizing secure shared‑responsibility models for AI/ML platforms and product teams consuming them, ensuring security and compliance are enforced by default through platform‑level and product‑level controls (e.g., model and agent access restrictions, data boundary enforcement, secure prompt and output handling, model lifecycle governance, and human‑in‑the‑loop controls)
[...]
- Continuously improve security processes and ways of working across the Data & AI Platform, reducing friction and bottlenecks by leveraging AI and automation to deliver measurable efficiency gains (e.g., faster throughput, improved consistency, reduced handoffs, and recovered engineering capacity)

results.title: Philip Morris International U.S. is hiring: Sr. Manager CX Loyalty & Retention - Stamford, CT, USA
highlights:
Philip Morris International U.S. is hiring: Sr. Manager CX Loyalty & Retention - Stamford, CT, USA
[...]
Home» Articles» Philip Morris International U.S. is hiring: Sr. Manager CX Loyalty & Retention – Stamford, CT, USA
[...]
# CAREERS & JOBS (LOYALTY & CX)Philip Morris International U.S. is hiring: Sr. Manager CX Loyalty & Retention – Stamford, CT, USA
[...]
As the department leader for retention and loyalty, this position provides strategic direction and people leadership to the CX Retention & Loyalty Manager, and CX Manager VOC & Product Care, while serving as the senior point of integration across Marketing, Digital, Analytics, IT, Finance, Legal, Compliance, and Field Operations. The Sr. Manager of Retention & Loyalty establishes governance, roadmaps, and prioritization to scale programs nationally, adapt to state-specific regulatory requirements, and modernize the retention ecosystem through advanced analytics, machine learning, and AI-driven personalization.
[...]
This leader is accountable for translating consumer insights into a differentiated value proposition, building a test-and-learn culture, and ensuring that data, technology, and partner capabilities are aligned to deliver a seamless, compliant, and high-performing consumer experience.
[...]
- Establish enterprise frameworks for segmentation, personalization, and engagement, including
[...]
prediction, and
[...]
- Drive alignment across Consumer Experience, Marketing, Digital/Product, Analytics/Data Science, IT/PMI Tech, Finance, Legal, Compliance, and Field Operations to deliver integrated journey-based initiatives.
[...]
- Ensure mystery shopping, sentiment analysis, trade-in, and sustainability programs are executed in accordance with regulations and that any AI-enabled decisioning is auditable, explainable where required, and appropriately governed.
[...]
- Lead adoption of AI/ML capabilities (churn and propensity modeling, uplift modeling, recommendation/next-best-action, offer optimization) and GenAI-enabled content operations (personalized copy variants, knowledge assist for agents) in partnership with Analytics and IT.
[...]
- Partner with Analytics/Data Science to design experiments and causal measurement (incrementality, lift, attribution where applicable) and to identify optimization opportunities across journeys and segments.
[...]
- Define AI/ML use cases and requirements for retention and loyalty (churn, propensity, uplift, recommendations, dynamic offers) and partner with Data Science to move models from prototype to production.
[...]
- Establish retention & loyalty governance for commercial integrated plan decisioning (taxonomy, data quality SLAs, consent signals, model documentation) and ensure responsible use of AI in consumer-facing experiences.
[...]
- Fluency in advanced analytics and applied ML/AI for growth (churn/propensity, uplift, recommendations, segmentation, experimentation) and ability to translate technical outputs into business actions.

results.title: Philip Morris International (PMI) South Africa Internships 2026 – Careerkick24.Com
highlights:
Philip Morris International (PMI) South Africa Internships 2026 – Careerkick24.Com

Philip Morris International invites ambitious and commercially minded South African graduates to apply for the Early Careers Internship Programme 2026.

Internship Location: South Africa

## About the Philip Morris International Internship Programme

Philip Morris International (PMI) is offering internship opportunities to graduates who are eager to gain practical workplace experience within a global organization.

The programme provides interns with hands-on exposure, professional development opportunities, and valuable industry experience while contributing to business growth and innovation.

## Available Internship Opportunities

### Key Accounts Administrator Intern

#### Requirements

Completed Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in:

- Business
- Commerce
- Marketing
- Finance
- Supply Chain
- Related fields

### Trade Marketing Coordinator Intern

#### Requirements

- Completed National Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree (non-negotiable)

### Human Resources (HR) Intern

#### Requirements

Completed National Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in:

- Human Resources Management
- Industrial Psychology

### Brand Management Intern

#### Requirements

- Completed National Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree (non-negotiable)

### IBP Intern – Supply Chain Analytics & Automation

#### Requirements

Completed Degree or National Diploma in:

- Data Science
- Industrial Engineering
- Systems Engineering
- Computer Science
- Supply Chain Management
- Operations Management
- Applied Mathematics
- Statistics
- Information Systems
- Related fields

## What PMI Offers Interns

Successful candidates will benefit from:

- Real workplace experience
- Exposure to global business operations
- Professional development opportunities
- Skills development and mentoring
- Career-building opportunities within an international company
- Collaborative and innovative work environment

## Who Should Apply?

PMI is looking for graduates who are:

- Curious and eager to learn
- Commercially minded
- Self-motivated and ambitious
- Strong communicators
- Interested in building a career within a multinational company

## How to Apply

B. Use the links given below to Apply

### Key Accounts Administrator Intern

### Apply Online for the Key Accounts Administrator Internship

### Trade Marketing Coordinator Intern

### Apply Online for the Trade Marketing Coordinator Internship

---

### HR Intern

### Apply Online for the HR Internship

---

### Brand Management Intern

---

### IBP Intern – Supply Chain Analytics & Automation

Apply Online for the IBP – Supply Chain Analytics & Automation Internship

Applications are open while positions remain available.

Get daily updates on jobs, internships, learnerships and career opportunities.

results.title: PMI U.S. Expands Its Talent Pipeline and Welcomes New Class of Interns Across the Enterprise
highlights:
STAMFORD, Conn., May 18, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Today, Philip Morris International's U.S. business (PMI U.S.) announced expanded internship and early‑career programs across the organization, underscoring its commitment to developing the next generation of talent to grow the business and innovate its industry. The programs are designed to provide meaningful experience, exposure, and opportunity for students just beginning their careers and early-career professionals ready to accelerate their impact.
[...]
This summer, PMI U.S. will welcome its second internship class, with roles across multiple geographies and functions. The program embeds interns directly into teams where they are immersed in meaningful, real‑world work from day one.
[...]
Proven Pathways from Internship to Full-Time CareersPMI U.S. invests in talent early and intentionally, providing hands‑on experience, leadership exposure, and clear pathways to growth.
[...]
Summer InternshipsThe Summer Internship Program offers 10 weeks of immersive experience to rising college seniors aged 21+. Interns work within core business functions—including finance, sales, operations, and more—and collaborate across teams on a capstone project, presenting their work to leaders at the conclusion of the program.
[...]
Early-Career Territory Sales ManagersA dedicated Early-Career Territory Sales Manager pathway offers professionals with less than three years of experience an opportunity to manage sales and service within assigned territories, working directly with retail partners to expand distribution, execute promotions, and merchandise products.
[...]
Fellows ProgramLaunched as a pilot in 2025, the Fellows Program is an immersive development experience designed for ambitious professionals with two or more years of work experience who are ready to accelerate their growth. Over 18–24 months, Fellows work closely with senior leaders, gaining exposure to strategy, leadership, culture, and execution across the enterprise. Through rotational assignments and project ownership, Fellows help shape the future of the business while building the skills needed for long‑term success.
[...]
PMI U.S.'
[...]
measurable results. Twenty‑five percent of the
[...]
5 intern class accepted offers to join the business full‑time following graduation, reinforcing the program as a strategic pipeline for long‑term talent development.
[...]
Invested in PeoplePMI U.S. invests in people, skills, and leadership capabilities to support future innovation and performance. This commitment to workplace excellence is reflected in a series of third‑party recognitions over the past year. The business earned Great Place to Work® Certification and was named a USA Today Top Workplace across multiple categories, including Professional Development and Employee Well‑Being. It was also included on Forbes' inaugural list of America's Best Companies to Work For & Invest In and ranked among The Wall Street Journal's Top 10 Best Managed Companies, highlighting the business's strong leadership, culture, and operational performance. To explore career opportunities at PMI U.S., visit Careers | Philip Morris International U.S.
[...]
PMI U.S.: Invested in AmericaPhilip Morris International Inc.'s U.S. businesses are invested in America's future and advancing a smoke-free nation. The businesses are committed to providing the approximately 25 million legal-age consumers who smoke cigarettes with better, smoke-free alternatives and to ensuring the products are marketed responsibly. From PMI's global headquarters in Stamford, Connecticut, and other locations nationwide, PMI U.S. contributes leadership, jobs, investment, and innovation in the U.S. The U.S. businesses employ more than 3,000 people across America and operate product manufacturing facilities, including in Aurora, Colorado, Owensboro, Kentucky, and Wilson, North Carolina. For more information, please

uspmi.com.

# Junior Trade Marketing & Visibility Manager
[...]
Philip Morris International • Germany
[...]
Lead the development and execution of multi-category trade marketing strategies for indirect channels, optimizing visual merchandising and POS materials across retail environments. Manage stakeholder coordination, project logistics, and temporary promotional campaigns to drive brand visibility and measurable market impact. Requires 3+ years of sales/marketing experience, strong Excel/PowerPoint skills, and fluent German/English communication.
[...]
###### Key Responsibilities
[...]
Support development and implementation of multi-category trade marketing strategy for indirect trade
[...]
Further develop existing and new advertising tools and POS materials for optimal product promotion
[...]
Optimize visual merchandising at Point of Sale across all relevant channels (specialty retail, gas stations, grocery retail)
[...]
Concept, internal alignment and execution of temporary advertising materials for campaigns and product launches
[...]
Prepare, coordinate and logistically equip marketing actions (e.g., CHEP actions, sales bundles, promotions)
[...]
Active stakeholder management with internal interfaces (PMI & PMG) and external coordination with agencies and service providers
[...]

- ZUM NÄCHSTMÖGLICHEN ZEITPUNKT AM STANDORT MÜNCHEN -Unsere Vision? Eine rauchfreie Zukunft! Dafür durchlaufen wir die größte Transformation unserer Firmengeschichte. Unser Ziel ist es, mit innovativen und disruptiven Produkten wie dem Tabakerhitzer IQOS herkömmliche Zigaretten durch schadstoffreduzierte Alternativen zu ersetzen. Warum wir das tun? Weil wir Worten Taten folgen lassen. Und DU kannst unseren Wandel mitgestalten. Nutze dieses einzigartige Momentum für den Einstieg bei uns als Junior Trade Marketing & Visibility Manager (m/w/d).Was Wir Dir Bieten
[...]
Als Visibility (Senior) Executive bringst du unsere Marken dorthin, wo Kaufentscheidungen entstehen: an den Point of Sale. Mit kreativen POS‑ und Werbekonzepten, enger Zusammenarbeit mit starken Partnern und viel Gestaltungsspielraum sorgst du für echte Sichtbarkeit und messbaren Impact im Markt.Dein Verantwortungsbereich

- Unterstützung bei der Entwicklung und Umsetzung einer Multi-Category-Trade-Marketing-Strategie für den indirekten Handel

- (Weiter-)Entwicklung bestehender und neuer Werbetools und POS-Materialien zur optimalen Vermarktung unserer Produkte

- Aktives Stakeholder-Management mit internen Schnittstellen (PMI & PMG) sowie externe Abstimmung mit Agenturen und Dienstleistern

- Abgeschlossenes wirtschaftswissenschaftliches Studium (z. B. BWL, Marketing, Sales)
- Mindestens 3 Jahre relevante Berufserfahrung im Sales- oder Marketingumfeld, idealerweise im FMCG-Bereich
- Fundierte Kenntnisse im Trade Marketing und ein gutes Verständnis der Handelslandschaft und ihrer Anforderungen

- Erfahrung im Projektmanagement sowie in der Steuerung externer Partner

- Sicherer Umgang mit MS Office, insbesondere Excel und PowerPoint
- Verhandlungssichere Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse

results.title: Philip Morris Digital Content Internship 2026: Applicants Must Show Strong Urge to Learn
highlights:
About Philip Morris Philip Morris South Africa is part of Philip Morris International, a leading global tobacco company focused on transforming its business toward a smoke-free future. In South Africa, the organisation is involved in introducing and expanding reduced-risk alternatives to traditional cigarettes while continuing to operate within a highly regulated market. The company places strong emphasis on innovation, scientific research, and responsible corporate practices as it works toward long-term public health and sustainability goals.
[...]
About the Philip Morris Digital Content Internship 2026 Philip Morris is currently searching for candidates who would like to obtain practical work exposure in the Digital Content field with their internship. The Philip Morris Digital Content Internship is designed as an immersive, hands-on experience rather than a passive, observational role. As part of PMI South Africa’s Early Careers Programme, interns are given direct exposure to the dynamic field of digital marketing within one of the world’s leading multinational FMCG companies.
[...]
From the outset, participants contribute to real projects, collaborate with experienced professionals, and actively participate in digital initiatives within a fast-paced organisation that values innovation and challenges conventional thinking. This opportunity is well-suited to individuals who are passionate about content creation, digital storytelling, social media, and developing their careers in a high-performance and growth-oriented environment.
[...]
- Gain hands-on experience in creating content for digital and social media platforms
[...]
- Receive exposure to the full lifecycle of digital campaign planning and execution, from concept to delivery
[...]
- Develop foundational competencies in content creation, community engagement, digital analytics, and campaign coordination
[...]
- Gain exposure to multiple digital channels, including social media, eCRM, websites, and paid media platforms
[...]
Eligibility Criteria Individuals interested in joining the Philip Morris Digital Content Internship are encouraged to submit an application. Applicants who are uncertain about their suitability for the role are advised to review the eligibility requirements below and compare their qualifications against the standards established by the selection committee. To maximize their chances of progressing through the recruitment process, candidates should ensure that they meet all stated criteria.
[...]
The Philip Morris Digital Content Internship Eligibility Criteria are as follows:
[...]
- Hold a completed National Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Digital Marketing, Communications, Media Studies, Journalism, or a related discipline
- Demonstrate no more than two years of relevant professional work experience
[...]
- Communicate fluently in English, both verbally and in writing
[...]
- Be available to commence the internship programme immediately upon
[...]
- Commit to participating in the internship programme on a full-time basis for a period of 12 months.
[...]
- Hold an Honours or Master’s Degree in a relevant field of study
- Demonstrate previous internship, freelance, campus, or professional experience related to social media management, content creation, marketing, communications, or digital campaign execution
[...]
- Demonstrate a strong capacity for learning, adaptability, and intellectual curiosity
- Communicate effectively through exceptional written and verbal communication skills
[...]
- Apply creative thinking to develop engaging and impactful content
[...]
ibilities and challenges
[...]
- Demonstrate a foundational understanding of digital marketing platforms, social media channels, and applicable content guidelines
[...]
- Show a keen interest in emerging digital trends, consumer behavior, and content performance analysis.
[...]
Application Instructions Apply online

results.title: Tobacco company to host PMI's global generative AI factory.
highlights:
Tobacco company to host PMI's global generative AI factory.
[...]
# Tabaqueira will host Philip Morris International's global generative AI factory in Portugal.
[...]
Tabaqueira was chosen by Philip Morris International (PMI) to host its new generative artificial intelligence factory, reinforcing the country's role as a strategic hub for innovation and technology within the group.
[...]
The new AI Factory will operate at the Tabaqueira plant in Albarraque and will serve PMI's global operations from Portugal. The operation will be led by Tomi Virkki. Head of AI Factory from PMI, which will coordinate an international team of engineers dedicated to the development and implementation of generative artificial intelligence solutions.
[...]
AI Factory will have its own PMI team, focused on leadership and operations, complemented by an implementation partner responsible for providing scalable specialized knowledge. Depending on project needs, the operation may involve around 100 specialists.
[...]
According to the company, this project represents a significant step in PMI's commitment to technological innovation and digital transformation, positioning Tabaqueira as a global leader in the development of solutions based on generative artificial intelligence.
[...]
The mission of the new AI Factory is to drive the development of AI tools and applications for different PMI business areas worldwide. Solutions could range from optimizing industrial processes to personalizing consumer experiences.

“The creation of the AI ​​Factory in Portugal represents a milestone in PMI’s technological transformation. We are bringing together cutting-edge talent and knowledge to develop generative artificial intelligence solutions that will have a global impact across various areas of our business. It is a privilege to lead this initiative from Portugal, a country that has demonstrated enormous capacity for innovation and technological excellence.”, says Tomi Virkki.

the country on the global map of digital innovation.".

Founded in 1927 by Alfredo da Silva and a subsidiary of Philip Morris International since 1997, Tabaqueira is the largest Portuguese tobacco company. results.title: Culture Becomes Real in Moments of Crisis”: Sarah Zaman of PMI on Leadership, AI, and Transformation | Times Now
highlights:
Becomes Real
[...]
of PMI on
[...]
, AI, and Transformation
[...]
”: Sarah Zaman of PMI on
[...]
, AI,
[...]
# Culture Becomes Real in Moments of Crisis”: Sarah Zaman of PMI on Leadership, AI, and Transformation
[...]
Leadership today is no longer just about managing people, it is about navigating uncertainty, adapting to rapid change and balancing technology with human judgment. For Sarah Zaman, Vice President, Regional People & Culture, South Asia, Indochina, CIS & MEA, Philip Morris International, the future workplace will belong to organisations that combine agility with authenticity while keeping people at the centre of transformation.
[...]
In a conversation on leadership, AI and organisational culture, Zaman reflected on how businesses must evolve to remain future-ready in an increasingly disrupted world.
[...]
Q. As workplaces become increasingly data-driven and AI-led, what does the future workforce look like to you?
[...]
Data-driven decision-making is critical, but human judgment will always remain central. Data gives inputs, but leadership still requires interpretation, instinct and context. If organisations rely only on numbers, they risk falling into analysis paralysis.
[...]
AI and digitalisation will absolutely reshape how work gets done, but I don’t see humans being replaced. I see humans being elevated toward higher-value work. The future of HR is no longer just about managing people; it’s about managing work itself, whether that work is done through people, automation or AI.
[...]
That’s why adaptability and resilience are becoming core capabilities for the workforce of the future.
[...]
At PMI, we’re actively experimenting with AI use cases globally while investing heavily in helping employees become future-ready rather than fear change. Workforce planning today is no longer just about hiring, it’s about understanding how technology and evolving business models will reshape jobs themselves.
[...]
India is especially important in that conversation. We see extraordinary talent here, and we want India not just to build talent for India, but talent for the world. Global mobility and cross-market exposure are becoming increasingly important because leadership today requires agility, adaptability and global perspective.

results.title: Tabaqueira escolhida para acolher Fábric... | Revista Líder
highlights:
Home Nacional Notícias Tabaqueira escolhida para acolher Fábrica de Inteligência Artificial Generativa da Philip Morris International em Portugal
[...]
# Tabaqueira escolhida para acolher Fábrica de Inteligência Artificial Generativa da Philip Morris International em Portugal
[...]
A Tabaqueira foi escolhida pela Philip Morris International para acolher a nova Fábrica de Inteligência Artificial Generativa (GenAI Factory), um projeto que reforça a aposta do grupo na transformação digital e consolida Portugal como um dos seus principais centros globais de inovação.
[...]
A unidade ficará instalada em Albarraque e terá impacto em todas as operações internacionais da PMI. A nova estrutura vai desenvolver ferramentas e aplicações baseadas em Inteligência Artificial Generativa para diferentes áreas de negócio, desde a otimização de processos industriais até à personalização de experiências de consumidor.
[...]
A liderança da AI Factory será assegurada por Tomi Virkki, Head of AI, que irá coordenar uma equipa internacional de engenheiros e especialistas em inteligência artificial. O projeto contará com uma equipa central da PMI focada em liderança e operação, sendo complementado por parceiros de implementação externos, capazes de mobilizar até cerca de 100 especialistas, consoante as necessidades dos projetos.
[...]
Segundo a empresa, a AI Factory assume-se como uma estrutura estratégica para acelerar a adoção de soluções de IA em escala global, integrando conhecimento técnico, engenharia de software e ciência de dados num único centro de desenvolvimento.
[...]
«Estamos a reunir talento e conhecimento de ponta para desenvolver soluções de Inteligência Artificial Generativa com impacto global», afirmou Tomi Virkki, sublinhando o papel de Portugal como base de operações para esta nova fase tecnológica da PMI.
[...]
Também o diretor-geral da Tabaqueira, Marcelo Nico, destacou o significado da decisão: «A escolha de Portugal para acolher a AI Factory é um reconhecimento claro do talento e da capacidade de inovação que temos vindo a demonstrar. Este projeto reforça o nosso compromisso com um ecossistema tecnológico mais robusto e competitivo»
[...]
Com esta decisão, a Tabaqueira prevê ainda aprofundar parcerias com universidades e empresas nacionais, procurando reforçar o ecossistema tecnológico em Portugal e criar novas oportunidades de desenvolvimento.
[...]
A PMI já mantém uma presença tecnológica relevante no país, com dois centros de serviços globais: o PMI Tech Hub Portugal e o Finance Hub. Atualmente, cerca de 26% da força de trabalho da Tabaqueira está integrada nestes hubs, desempenhando funções altamente qualificadas em áreas como tecnologia, cibersegurança, inteligência artificial, gestão de dados e finanças globais.
[...]
O Tech Hub Portugal é já o segundo maior centro tecnológico da PMI a nível mundial, com mais de 250 especialistas. O Finance Hub, por sua vez, assegura operações financeiras em 180 mercados, reforçando o papel de Portugal como plataforma estratégica para a multinacional.

results.title: Tabaqueira acolhe nova fábrica global de Inteligência Artificial da PMI | Sintra Notícias

Tabaqueira acolhe nova fábrica global de Inteligência Artificial da PMI

A Tabaqueira foi escolhida pela Philip Morris International (PMI) para acolher a nova Fábrica de Inteligência Artificial Generativa (GenAI) do grupo, consolidando Portugal como um centro estratégico de inovação e tecnologia à escala global.

A nova AI Factory ficará instalada na unidade da empresa em Albarraque, na freguesia de Rio de Mouro, em Sintra, e irá apoiar, a partir de Portugal, todas as operações internacionais da PMI.

A operação será liderada por Tomi Virkki, Head of AI da PMI, que ficará responsável pela coordenação de uma equipa internacional de engenheiros dedicada ao desenvolvimento e implementação de soluções de Inteligência Artificial Generativa para os mercados globais do grupo.

A estrutura da AI Factory assenta numa equipa da PMI focada na liderança e gestão operacional, complementada por um parceiro de implementação especializado, responsável por disponibilizar competências técnicas escaláveis. Dependendo das necessidades de cada projeto, a operação poderá envolver cerca de uma centena de especialistas em inteligência artificial.

- Contactos
- Estatuto editorial e ficha técnica
- Conduta dos utilizadores

.

results.title: Devs.com.pt | Lançamento da Fábrica de IA Philip Morris Portugal 2026
highlights:
Devs.com.pt | Lançamento da Fábrica de IA Philip Morris Portugal 2026

# Philip Morris AI Factory Portugal 2026: Um Novo Centro de Inovação Global

A Philip Morris está a expandir a sua configuração digital no Sul da Europa. A nova Philip Morris fábrica de IA Portugal 2026 é um grande passo para a região. Esta unidade especial constrói aplicações inteligentes para áreas de negócio globais. Segundo as últimas notícias da plataforma, este movimento coloca o país como um centro tecnológico de topo. O novo escritório coordena projetos de dados profundos diretamente da Europa.

## Instalação de AI Generativa da PMI Tabaqueira Amplia Pegada Operacional

A empresa global coloca a sua equipa de tecnologia dentro de uma fábrica real. O centro de IA generativa da PMI Tabaqueira opera em Albarraque. Os principais engenheiros de dados gerem o trabalho diário lá. A equipa poderá crescer para 100 especialistas em tecnologia em breve. Muitas marcas globais estão a atualizar o seu software neste momento. Este novo centro suporta grandes projetos sem atrasos.

## Tendências de Inovação na Distribuição de Software GenAI Empresarial na Europa

Grandes empresas adotam novo software por toda a Europa hoje. Esta massiva tendência de distribuição de software GenAI empresarial na Europa ajuda as equipas a trabalharem muito mais rápido. As equipas tecnológicas concentram-se em construir ferramentas inteligentes. Estas ferramentas analisam dados e ajudam os líderes a tomar decisões. As principais empresas de tecnologia globais acompanham esta fábrica de perto. O projeto utiliza tanto líderes internos como parceiros tecnológicos.

## Otimização de Processos Industriais Estudo de Caso de AI Generativa

Fábricas antigas estão a mudar os seus modelos de trabalho. Novas ferramentas de otimização de processos industriais com AI generativa automatizam tarefas de escritório difíceis. A região atrai muitos centros tecnológicos hoje. Estes escritórios trabalham em configurações na nuvem e cibersegurança. Esta fábrica coloca Albarraque no mapa tecnológico global. Mostra como as fábricas antigas se tornam centros digitais modernos.

## Related News

### Amdocs Aumenta a Automação Global em Telecomunicações com a Aquisição da Yess

### Luís Ribeiro KLx Entrevista 2026: Elevar o Papel dos Engenheiros de Software Arrastar

results.title: Philip Morris brings the Swedish model to the center of Technovation
highlights:
Philip Morris brings the Swedish model to the center of Technovation
[...]
It is no surprise that a packed agenda of meetings and panels took place on May 19th and 20th in Stockholm during the Technovation, the event organized by PMI that brought together policymakers, scientists, journalists, researchers, regulators, and industry representatives to discuss the future of nicotine consumption. From the science of oral products to regulatory policies, including the role of consumer education and technological innovation, the common thread throughout the two days was the so-called "Swedish model."
[...]
As per tradition, the proceedings were opened by Tommaso Di Giovanni, Vice President Communications and Engagement of Philip Morris, which immediately brought the debate back to one of the central themes of the event: the difficulty with which governments and societies tend to embrace change and the role that science should have in guiding it.
[...]
In this regard, Di Giovanni cited what's happening with synthetic meat, autonomous driving, wearable devices capable of monitoring vital signs, and, similarly, with innovations such as heated tobacco devices, e-vapors, and nicotine pouches. This reticence isn't stopping PMI, which has been investing since 2008. 16 billion dollars in research and development for new combustion-free devices and has employed over 1.600 researchers and engineers.
[...]
Of course, despite the friction, the evolutionary process has been underway for several years now, and some countries, such as the United Kingdom and Sweden, are adopting increasingly open approaches to combustion-free products for adult smokers who want to give up traditional cigarettes. These devices and alternatives continue to evolve, thanks also to technological development and scientific research. On this basis, Technovation, an event dedicated specifically to this topic, opened in Stockholm. relationship between innovation, technology and the future of nicotine consumption.
[...]
A theme that also directly connects to the multi-category strategy of PMI, increasingly focused on a variety of smoke-free products, from IQOS heated tobacco devices to VEEV e-vapors to ZYN nicotine pouches. This direction was also strengthened by the acquisition of Swedish Match in 2022 which, as underlined by Tommaso Di Giovanni, "It didn't just mean acquiring a company, but also knowledge” in one of the most advanced markets in the world in the oral nicotine products sector.
[...]
The first day of Technovation focused primarily on the relationship between science, regulation, and smoke-free products, alternating between technical panels, policy meetings, and immersive experiences dedicated to SMEs' vast smoke-free portfolio. After the opening address by Tommaso Di Giovanni, much of the attention shifted to nicotine substitutes and on their decisive role in reducing cigarette consumption.
[...]
Among the most relevant moments is also the comparison between Christos Harpantidis, Group Chief Corporate Affairs Officer of PMI, and Nick Ricketts, CEO of Swedish Match AB and President of Oral Products at PMI, dedicated specifically to the future prospects of the smoke-free market and the lessons learned from the Swedish case. Ample space was also given to the scientific aspects of nicotine, with presentations focusing on addiction, relative risk, and toxicological data.
[...]
by figures from the healthcare, institutional and regulatory world such as Ellen Huttunen, Finnish advisor on health policy,
[...]
Agnieszka Wyszynska
[...]
. The speakers
[...]
too restrictive or poorly
[...]
## Philip Morris Technovation: Overcoming Myths for a Smoke-Free Future

From STEM Skills to Industry: The Philip Morris Model
[...]
Dialogue is crucial. If we truly

we need a debate

results.title: Doctors file a complaint against Philip Morris for 'misleading AI tool': 'This is not a citizens' initiative but a carefully designed influence campaign' - World News | TakeToNews
highlights:
A group of around fifteen hundred doctors, supported by The Smoke-Free Generation (the Heart Foundation, KWF Cancer Control and the Lung Fund), filed a complaint with the Advertising Code Committee on Friday against a “misleading AI campaign” by tobacco manufacturer Philip Morris.
[...]
The user only has to click on objection points and fill them in themselves, and with the help of artificial intelligence, an argument is then produced that can be sent to the online consultation of the European Commission for the revision of the European Tobacco Products Directive. “Offered by Philip Morris Benelux BV” is written in smaller font below the QR code.
[...]
Moreover, it is not allowed, thinks lawyer Laura van Gijn, who drew up the complaint on behalf of the doctors. “We believe that this qualifies as tobacco advertising. And that is prohibited. It is presented as a kind of citizens’ initiative, but it is a form of influence, in which all kinds of arguments are fabricated that suit Philip Morris very well. Heated tobacco is also presented as a less harmful alternative.” Philip Morris has recently been investing in an electronic device that heats tobacco instead of burning it.
[...]
Van Gijn: “For example, if you manually enter: ‘I want a ban, ban, ban. And I am in favor of further restrictions’, the tool generates a story in which it automatically says things like: ‘It is time to shift the focus to less harmful alternatives’. Philip Morris is a dominant player in heated tobacco worldwide, and that is exactly the market in which they are looking for growth. They mislead citizens and then put them in front of their lobby. The consumer also hears all kinds of positive things about products. that Philip Morris sells.”
[...]
If NRC puts it to the test and clicks on using tobacco in the tool, then checks ‘traditional cigarettes’ as the most important category and when asked ‘Which aspects of the new rules are most important to you?’ If you check the ‘freedom of choice’ box, the question follows: ‘What else are you specifically concerned about?’ The answer options: ‘A diverse range of alternatives increases the chance of a successful switch to smoke-free alternatives’ and ‘having freedom of choice’. After choosing the first option, NRC explicitly fills in the free section: ‘A total ban would help me get rid of my addiction.’
[...]
The language model makes
[...]
clear: “I believe that the European Union’s Tobacco Products Directive should allow a wide range of options […]. B
[...]
everything wouldn’t solve the problem; it would only drive people back to the more harmful traditional options.
[...]
need choices, not restrictions. […] Removing all options is not the
[...]
everything away.” One click and the
[...]
to the European Commission.
[...]
“That is why we want the Advertising Code Committee to classify this as tobacco advertising and as a misleading practice,” says lawyer Van Gijn. “If they do that, Philip Morris will really be in trouble. As far as I’m concerned, we will then go to civil court.”
[...]
Philip Morris responds to questions from NRC An written explanation on ‘Project Voice’. It points out ‘the right of individuals and stakeholders to contribute to legislative initiativesthat could affect them.” The company says it will inform consumers at tobacco points of sale “about the opportunity to share their opinions.” “This is emphatically not an advertisement for products, nor a call for a prescribed position, as users are free to write whatever they want.”
[...]
The Smoke-Free Generation supports the doctors’ complaint. The partnership has filed a complaint about the Philip Morris campaign with the Dutch Food and Consumer Product Safety Authority (NVWA). The initiators also report this to the Dutch Data Protection Authority, because the interface of the website is not in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

results.title: Generative AI Architect - YER
highlights:
Generative AI Architect - YER
[...]
# Generative AI Architect
[...]
Build the next generation of AI-enabled engineering workflows in wireless connectivity
[...]
Within Philips IP&S, we are shaping how Generative AI transforms complex, knowledge-intensive engineering domains.
[...]
We are looking for a Generative AI Architect to help design and build AI-powered workflows for our Wireless Connectivity Standardization team, working across 5G, 6G, Wi-Fi, and future connectivity standards.
[...]
This role sits at the intersection of Generative AI, knowledge systems, and advanced engineering workflows, where the goal is not experimentation; but real adoption in high-impact technical processes.
[...]
The Role: AI applied to high-stakes engineering workflows
[...]
You will design and implement GenAI-driven systems that enhance how experts work, rather than replacing them.
[...]
- Accelerate understanding of complex technical documentation
- Improve consistency and quality of structured outputs
- Support IP and standardization workflows with AI-assisted automation
- Enable knowledge retrieval and reuse across large document ecosystems
[...]
A core principle in this role is human-in-the-loop AI design, where domain experts remain in control, and AI acts as an intelligent augmentation layer.
[...]
You will work closely with wireless connectivity experts and engineering stakeholders to translate high-value use cases into scalable AI solutions.
[...]
Develop workflow automation for IP lifecycle and standardization processes, including:
[...]
Prototype advanced AI capabilities such as:
[...]
- intelligent document retrieval across large technical corpora
- topic identification and semantic clustering
- AI-assisted document improvement and refinement
[...]
We are looking for someone who operates comfortably at the intersection of AI engineering and applied system design.
[...]
Hands-on expertise in:
[...]
- LLM-based application design
- Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems
- Agentic AI / orchestration frameworks
- Prompt and context engineering at system level
- AI evaluation methodologies and quality frameworks
[...]
- Experience in telecom, wireless systems, semiconductor, or standardization environments is highly valued
- AI directly impacts engineering output quality and speed
- Knowledge systems are mission-critical and highly specialized
- Reliability and correctness matter as much as innovation
- AI is embedded into structured technical workflows, not standalone tools
[...]
You will help define how Generative AI becomes a repeatable capability inside engineering-driven organizations, rather than isolated experimentation.
[...]
Generative AI is rapidly changing how technical organizations work—but most implementations remain fragmented or experimental.
[...]
This role exists to bridge that gap by building:
[...]
- production-grade AI workflows
- reusable AI capabilities across teams
- scalable knowledge systems for wireless engineering domains
[...]
The goal is to move from AI experimentation → operational AI capability embedded in engineering workflows.
[...]
You will directly contribute to:
[...]
- Faster and higher-quality wireless standardization processes
- Improved consistency and reuse of technical knowledge
- Reduced manual effort in IP and documentation workflows
- Scalable GenAI capabilities that extend beyond a single team or use case
[...]
- A challenging key position within an ambitious and innovative organization, where you help shape (Gen)AI & ML innovation
- A dynamic, multicultural team of engineers, data scientists, and researchers working on real-world AI & ML at the highest level
- A permanent contract that combines stability with the freedom to put your own mark on the role
[...]
- Working in a close-knit, multidisciplinary, and entrepreneurial team where your vision truly matters
- International growth opportunities: development within leading-edge projects in AI, cloud, and software

results.title: Data Scientist, Medical Analytics, Care Gaps, Customer Segmentation | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist, Medical Analytics, Care Gaps, Customer Segmentation | Wfh

Wfh.← All Jobs

# Data Scientist, Medical Analytics, Care Gaps, Customer Segmentation

Posted Jun 4, 2026

- Job Description:
- Plan and perform care gap advanced analytics and segment HCPs and KOLs using real world data sources as input.
- Support team Product Manager in strategic and tactical planning with regards to medical analyses.
- Collaborate with team Product manager to communicate generated insights.
- Prepare and maintain datasets for on-going analytical needs in collaboration with data engineers.
- Flag data issues to data engineers and advise on required resolution.
- Develop integrated care gaps analyses planning including data input required.
- Plan, generate and perform asset-specific care gap analyses using advanced techniques such as predictive models, machine learning, probabilistic causal models, and unsupervised algorithms.
- Create asset-specific HCP segments, maps, and prioritizations to guide channel mix/activation, content creation, and MSL activity in Anchor countries.
- Highlight missing data inputs needed to conduct analytics (e.g., claims data for unmet need analysis).
- Ensure visualization / insights generation from data is easily accessible and understandable for users.

- Requirements:
- Degree in Data Sciences, or Computer Sciences, Statistics, Clinical Informatics or similar, with advanced qualification in Data Science.
- Relevant certification &/or work experience in data engineering.
- Experience (6+ years with Bachelors; 5+ with Masters; 1+ with PHD) in data sciences, notably in the healthcare sector.
- Demonstrated effectiveness working in cross-functional business environment.
- Proven influencing skills.
- Strong interpersonal skills to quickly build rapport and credibility with Pfizer leaders and key external stakeholders.
- Ability to partner cross culturally/regionally.
- Effective English verbal and written communication.
- In depth understanding of the business of pharmaceutical medicine including clinical trial design, GCP and data interpretation, drug development, regulatory and promotional rules/guidance, legal and compliance, issue management and business development opportunity evaluations.

- Benefits:
- Health benefits to include medical, prescription drug, dental and vision coverage.
- 401(k) plan with Pfizer Matching Contributions and an additional Pfizer Retirement Savings Contribution.
- Paid vacation, holiday and personal days.
- Paid caregiver/parental and medical leave.
- Comprehensive and generous benefits and programs to help colleagues lead healthy lives and support life’s moments.

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents) - Taipei | OpenTalent
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents) - Taipei | OpenTalent

All jobs
onsite
# Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents)
Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents)
Appier is seeking a Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents) to join their Enterprise Solution Science Team. In this role, you will be responsible for the end-to-end development, from problem definition to model deployment, of cutting-edge AI agents for marketing, strategy, sales, and service applications. You will collaborate with product and ML engineering teams, conduct evaluations, and stay updated with the latest research in LLM and agentic AI.
## About the role
## About the Role
We are looking for a **Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents)** to join the Enterprise Solution Science Team. This team focuses on applying cutting-edge ML technologies to real-world marketing problems by combining them with omnichannel customer data. In this role, you will be responsible for end-to-end development, from problem definition to driving model deployment into production.
## What You’ll Work On
* You will contribute to the design, development, and productionization of one or more enterprise AI agents, including:
* **Marketing Agent**: Empower marketers to improve conversion rates and drive revenue growth.
* **Strategy Agent**: Transform raw data into actionable, revenue-driving strategies, and high-potential audience segments.
* **Sales and Service Agent**: Drive sales conversion, increase average order value (AOV), deepen brand engagement and customer loyalty through real-time, 24/7 AI-powered support.
* Collaborate with product teams to translate product requirements into well-defined machine learning problems
* Work with ML engineers to deploy models into production environments
* Conduct offline and online evaluation, monitoring, and continuous optimization after deployment
* Stay up to date with the latest research and proactively propose innovative applications## What We’re Looking For (Minimum)
* Master’s or PhD degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics, Electrical Engineering, or related fields
* Experience in **machine learning**, **LLM**, or **software engineering** through research, internships, projects, or industry work
* Experience or strong interest in **LLM / agentic application development**, including:
* Skills / tools / function calling
* AI Protocol (MCP / A2A)
* Workflow orchestration
* Benchmark evaluation
* Strong mindset with analytical and problem-solving skills, and passion for learning cutting-edge technologies
* Familiarity with AI-powered development tools such as: **Claude Code**, **OpenAI Codex / Codex CLI**, **Cursor**, **Windsurf**, **GitHub Copilot**## What We’re Looking For (Preferred)
* Strong focus on quantitative metrics and systematic evaluation
* Industry experience in MarTech and passion for building customer-centric products
* Familiarity with agentic product development concepts such as:
* Context engineering
* Harness engineering
* Evaluation / testing workflows
* Human-in-the-loop workflows
Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents) - Taipei | OpenTalent

# Senior Software Engineer - AI/ML in Pharmaceutical Research
[...]
Design and implement production-grade full stack applications that integrate LLM and AI capabilities into scientific workflows. Collaborate with medicinal chemists and biomedical researchers to deliver intuitive, user-centric applications. Contribute to pioneering research in semantic frameworks and AI-driven knowledge systems.

Position SummaryIn this innovative role, you will combine cutting-edge software engineering with artificial intelligence to build next-generation knowledge work automation tools that directly impact pharmaceutical research outcomes.You will work at the forefront of applied AI, collaborating closely with Medicinal Chemists and Biomedical Scientists to design, develop, and deploy intelligent systems that enhance productivity and accelerate decision-making in drug discovery.This is a unique opportunity to bridge modern web technologies with Large Language Model (LLM) capabilities, creating intuitive user experiences that solve real-world scientific challenges. As you deliver core solutions, you'll have the exciting opportunity to contribute to pioneering research in semantic frameworks that benchmark and expand the understanding of concepts and semantic similarity in LLMs.Position Responsibilities
[...]
- Design and implementation of production-grade full stack applications that seamlessly integrate LLM and AI capabilities into scientific workflows, enabling researchers to leverage cutting-edge artificial intelligence in their daily work
[...]
- Development of scalable backend services using Python frameworks for data processing, embedding generation, vector search, and LLM orchestration that power AI-driven research tools
[...]
- Implementation of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, conversational AI interfaces, and agentic LLM architectures that automate knowledge work in pharmaceutical research
[...]
- Integration of semantic search technologies, vector databases and embedding models to enable intelligent information retrieval
[...]
- Contribution to the development of novel semantic frameworks and conceptual research that advances AI-driven knowledge systems for drug discovery
[...]
- Master’s degree and 2+ years of experience building AI powered research applications
[...]
- Background or demonstrated interest in life sciences, pharmaceutical research, drug discovery, or cheminformatics
[...]
- Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks and libraries (OpenAI API, Hugging Face Transformers, Anthropic Claude)
[...]
- Practical knowledge of prompt engineering, LLM optimization techniques, and best practices for building LLM-powered applications
[...]
- Experience building conversational AI interfaces, chatbots, or agentic systems with autonomous decision-making capabilities
[...]
- Familiarity with vector databases and semantic search technologies for similarity search and retrieval
[...]
- Demonstrated experience implementing, training, fine-tuning, or applying deep learning models to natural language processing or computer vision problems using PyTorch

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:
[...]
- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.
[...]
- Develop across the lifecycle of LLMs including data engineering, pre-training, fine-tuning, post-training, and evaluation
[...]
- Deploy models in production environments, working closely with other engineers to ensure scalability and reliability
[...]
- Solve core backend challenges in ML and engineering including the design, implementation, and scaling of our data, training, and deployment pipeline
[...]
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across both AIDD and Roche to solve
[...]
- You have a BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, related field, or equivalent experience and 2+ years of industry experience in machine learning; or a PhD in Computer Science and related field and 0+ years of industry experience.
[...]
- You have demonstrated success in technical capabilities deploying machine learning models in industry-grade production environments
[...]
- Experience in back-end development for LLM-powered agents and tools, or working with Model Context Protocol (MCP).
[...]
- You have experience in developing LLM/GenAI products and are familiar with open-source AI frameworks like Open WebUI.
[...]
- Strong interest in solving problems in drug discovery and biomedical science.
[...]
- You have strong programming skills in Python and using deep learning frameworks like PyTorch
[...]
- You have extensive experience with deep learning & LLMs.
[...]
in machine learning
[...]
- An extensive track record of delivering innovative solutions in machine learning

results.title: Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
highlights:
Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
[...]
Senior Data Scientist
[...]
🦸‍♀️ The Role 🦸‍♀️
[...]
Our mission is to help large successful brands like Uber, Amazon, Wise, HelloFresh (and more!) put their customers at the centre of everything they do. Using best-in-class tech in a fast-developing AI space, our Customer Experience Intelligence platform continuously analyses explicit and implicit feedback to enable our clients to identify what they should do next.
[...]
We’re hiring a Senior Data Scientist to join the team and help build and ship the next generation of that stack.
[...]
Unlike many companies, we use our own custom models, specialised for customer feedback, across various parts of the stack: extraction, retrieval, reranking, summarisation, and sentiment analysis. We are also pragmatic and understand that the right solution can be a combination of off-the-shelf LLMs, bespoke fine-tuned models, and sometimes techniques that utilise no LLM at all.
[...]
Train, evaluate, and iterate on ML models and agentic systems for customer feedback, including owning our custom fine-tuning pipelines. Run experiments end-to-end, track results rigorously, and make clear recommendations on what to ship, iterate, or retire.
[...]
Build and maintain LLM-powered features: retrieval pipelines, reranking systems, insight agents, data mining agents, and automated taxonomy generation.
[...]
Design and run robust evaluation frameworks: build test sets, define metrics, evaluate non-deterministic systems, handle class imbalance, and automate checkpoint comparisons.
[...]
Improve and extend semantic search and retrieval, evolving from embedding-based approaches toward more advanced methods.
[...]
Write production-quality code and collaborate closely with Engineering on productionisation, model serving, data pipelines, and monitoring.
[...]
Work with Product and Commercial teams to translate business needs into practical ML solutions, and support client evaluations and accuracy benchmarking.
[...]
🧰 What you’ll need:
[...]
A deep working knowledge of transformer architectures.
[...]
Strong PyTorch skills, with the ability to write custom training loops, modify model architectures, and debug issues at the tensor level. Ideally, experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques such as LoRA
[...]
Extensive experience working with large-scale, messy real-world text data, including classification, extraction, embeddings, re-rankers, clustering, and search.
[...]
Experience in instruction fine-tuning and serving language models, familiarity with frameworks such as vLLM, DeepSpeed, or similar tools
[...]
A solid grounding in classical ML and statistics, and the judgement to choose simpler methods when they’re the right solution.
[...]
Practical experience building with GenAI and agentic patterns.
[...]
Technical curiosity and a keen interest in AI – a love of experimenting to make the most of available technology.
[...]
bonus if you
[...]
MSc/PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computational Linguistics or a closely related STEM field.
[...]
Experience with reinforcement learning techniques, such as with verifiable reward (RLVR)
[...]
#machinelearning #machinelearningscientist #seniormachinelearningscientist #datascience #deeplearning #LLM #ML #data #AI #PyTorch
[...]

[...]
Chattermill Analytics Limited
[...]

[...]
Senior (5+ years of experience)

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group, we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will contribute to our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at relevant drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Design, implement, and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, writing robust, performance-critical code and contributing to core infrastructure.
[...]
Model Improvement & Reasoning: Develop and execute strategies to systematically improve performance on scientific tasks, including long-horizon task completion and complex reasoning challenges.
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into concrete machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria.
[...]
Evaluation & Benchmarks: Design and implement evaluation methodologies to assess model capabilities relevant to biological research, working with domain experts to establish benchmarks and curate high-quality data.
[...]
Research-to-Production: Collaborate closely with researchers to translate ideas and prototypes into scalable, production-ready systems.
[...]
Focus: You focus on the execution of defined projects. You are responsible for writing clean, efficient code to test specific hypotheses regarding reasoning and alignment.
[...]
Engineering: You contribute to the maintenance of the training infrastructure and data pipelines, ensuring experiments run reliably on our clusters.
[...]
Collaboration: You work closely with senior scientists to implement novel algorithms, translating research papers into working prototypes.
[...]
BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, or a related quantitative field with 2+ years of relevant work experience. Or Ph. D. with 0-2 years relevant work experience.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including post-training techniques to enhance domain knowledge, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data).
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]
The expected salary range
[...]
this position,
[...]
the primary location of New York City, is $141
[...]

[...]

[...]
